response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar on paper — a technical bachelor's degree and roughly 19 years of software engineering experience spanning web, embedded, and low-latency systems — but the profile is that of a strong senior/staff-level generalist IC, not a director-level Principal Engineer driving AI strategy. AI exposure is recent and tooling-oriented (MCP code generators, agent infrastructure since 2025) rather than deep or recognized expertise, and there is no evidence of shaping product or platform strategy, advising executive-level customers, or influencing at organizational scope.
strengths: 19+ years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Genuine entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption
Active knowledge-sharing through conference talks, workshops, and courses, plus recent AI-agent/MCP infrastructure work
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML depth or recognized AI expertise; AI-related work is very recent and limited to agent tooling
Career trajectory tops out at senior/staff IC roles with no evidence of principal-level strategic or cross-organizational influence
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited cloud-platform strategy experience relative to the role's focus

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. role focus
Long, versatile engineering experience with entrepreneurial background
